content.text:
[Mon 2026-07-13 05:39 UTC] I am planning another F1 watch party and need help choosing two snacks from the four options I uploaded.

Please check for information about the F1 watch-party details, my earlier trail-mix idea, the store I buy it from, which I believe is referenced in my messages, but I forgot the name, and the gluten-free requirement registered in one of the previous events. These may be from different occasions, so keep them separate.

Compare the four products using the uploaded package photos, the product CSV, the event requirements, and the FDA guides. Check whether the package details match the CSV, what allergens are listed, whether the product clearly says gluten-free, and whether anything is missing, unclear, or inconsistent. Please keep "contains" and "may contain" warnings separate, and don't assume a product is gluten-free just because wheat is not listed.

Also, find out how many people attended the watch party, including me. For every product, read the labeled serving size and serving per container, then calculate its listed cost per serving. If the serving count or package size can not be verified from the images, mark the calculation as uncertain instead of guessing.

Use the trail-mix photo only to see which option is most similar. Then choose exactly two snack packs that are in stock, cost no more than $18 total, do not declare wheat, and have matching package sizes. At least one must clearly say gluten-free.

Each selected pack must provide at least one serving for every person at the party, and the two packs together must provide at least three servings per person. Prefer the better-value pair when more than one combination qualifies.

Save the full review as snack_packaging_audit.md and the final two choices as snack_purchase_plan.csv using these columns: candidate, product_name, listed_price_usd, serving_per_container, cost_per_serving_usd, visible_gluten_free, declared_contains, voluntary_warning, package_size_match, trail_mix_similarity, selection_reason.

# KIND Minis – Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt

---

## FRONT OF PACKAGE

**Brand/Product Line:** KIND minis

**Flavor:** Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt

**Front Claims:**
- 90 Calories
- #1 Ingredient **Almonds**
- 3g Sugar
- **GLUTEN FREE**
- With Almonds & Peanuts
- 10 Bars

**Package Size/Count:**
- 10 – 0.7 OZ (20g) BARS
- NET WT 7.0 OZ (200g)

**On individual bar wrapper shown:** "KIND minis" with tagline "INGREDIENTS YOU CAN SEE & PRONOUNCE"

---

## NUTRITION FACTS PANEL

- **Servings per carton:** 10
- **Serving Size:** 1 bar (20g)
- **Calories:** 90

| Nutrient | Amount | % Daily Value |
|---|---|---|
| **Total Fat** | 7g | 9% |

| Iron | 1mg | 6% |

*\* The % Daily Value tells you how much a nutrient in a serving of food contributes to a daily diet. 2,000 calories a day is used for general nutrition advice.*

---

## INGREDIENTS

Almonds, peanuts, chicory root fiber, honey, palm kernel oil, sugar, glucose syrup, rice flour, unsweetened chocolate, alkalized cocoa, sea salt, soy lecithin, natural flavor, cocoa butter.

---

## ALLERGEN STATEMENT

---

## CERTIFICATIONS

## COMPANY INFORMATION

Brought to you by KIND LLC
P.O. BOX 705 Midtown Station
NY, NY 10018 | +1 855.884.KIND

- Not a significant source of vitamin D, calcium and potassium.
- *The % Daily Value (DV) tells you how much a nutrient in a serving of food contributes to a daily diet. 2,000 calories a day is used for general nutrition advice.

---

## Ingredients:
Whole Grain Oats, Sugar, Canola and/or Sunflower Oil, Rice Flour, Honey, Salt, Brown Sugar Syrup, Baking Soda, Soy Lecithin, Natural Flavor.

# Chex Mix™ — Traditional Original Recipe Snack Mix

---

## Front of Package

**Product Name:** Chex Mix™ Snack Mix
**Variety:** Traditional — Original Recipe
**Promotional Claim:** "NOW Chex-tra Delicious™" / "AS COMPARED TO PREVIOUS RECIPE"
**Note:** "ENLARGED TO SHOW DETAIL"
**Gluten-Free Label:** **None visible** — no gluten-free claim is present on the front.
**Package Size:** Partially obscured at bottom; appears to read "NET WT 15 OZ (425g)" though the text is cut off/folded.
**Front nutritional callouts (bottom right corner):** 140 CALORIES and other partially visible per-serving info badges (partially cut off).

---

## Nutrition Facts

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| **Servings per container** | About 39 |
| **Serving size** | ½ cup (29g) |
| **Calories** | 120 |

| Total Sugars | 2g |
| Includes 2g Added Sugars | (4% DV) |
| **Protein** | 2g |
| **Iron** | 0.7mg (4% DV) |

**Statement:** "Not a significant source of vitamin D, calcium and potassium."

**Footnote:** "* The % Daily Value (DV) tells you how much a nutrient in a serving of food contributes to a daily diet. 2,000 calories a day is used for general nutrition advice."

---

## Ingredients

Whole Wheat, Degermed Yellow Corn Meal, Enriched Flour (wheat flour, niacin, iron, thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), Vegetable Oil (soybean, canola, and/or rice bran), Sugar. **Contains 2% or less of:** Salt, Rye Flour, Maltodextrin, Yeast, Spices, Color Added, Dextrose, Distilled Monoglycerides, Baking Soda, Trisodium Phosphate, Yellow Corn Flour, Calcium Carbonate, Autolyzed Yeast, Dried Onion, Dried Garlic, Hydrolyzed Soy Protein, Barley Malt Syrup, Citric Acid, Disodium Inosinate, Disodium Guanylate, Natural Flavor. **Freshness Preserved by BHT.**

---

## Allergen Statements

**"Contains" statement:** **CONTAINS WHEAT AND SOY INGREDIENTS.**

**"May contain" / "Manufactured in a facility" statement:** **None visible** on the portion of the package shown.

---

## Certifications

**None visible.** No gluten-free certification, no organic seal, no kosher symbol, no Non-GMO Project Verified logo, etc., are visible in these images.

---

## Additional Information

- **Distributed by:** General Mills Sales, Inc., Minneapolis, MN 55440 USA
- **Carbohydrate Choices:** 1 1/2
- **© General Mills** — Code: 3718158605

# Power Up Premium Trail Mix – Mega Omega

## Brand & Packaging
- **Brand:** Power Up
- **Product Line:** Premium Trail Mix
- **Variety:** Mega Omega
- The packaging features a **black and white resealable bag** with the Power Up logo (a diamond shape with an upward arrow)
- Certifications visible on the bag include what appears to be a **Non-GMO Project Verified** seal and a **Kosher (K)** symbol, along with an **OU** or **M** kosher marking
- The net weight is partially obscured by the person's hand

## Ingredients/Components
The bag clearly lists and illustrates **five key ingredients**:

1. **Cranberries** (dried) – visible as small red pieces
2. **Mango** (dried) – light yellow/golden chunks
3. **Almonds** – whole, skin-on almonds
4. **Walnuts** – walnut pieces/halves
5. **Pumpkin Seeds** (pepitas) – green-tinted seeds

## Type of Trail Mix
This is a **nuts, seeds, and dried fruit** trail mix — notably **no chocolate or candy** components. It's health-focused, emphasizing **omega fatty acids** (hence the "Mega Omega" name), which come from the walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and almonds. It's positioned as a **premium, wholesome snack** without artificial additions.

Through the clear window at the bottom of the bag, you can see the colorful mix of all five ingredients blended together.

[Gluten-Free Labeling of Foods Main Page](/food/allergens/gluten-free-labeling-foods)

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has defined the term “gluten-free” and established conditions for the voluntary use of the term in the labeling of foods. Any food product bearing a “gluten-free” claim must comply with our regulation at § 101.91 (21 CFR 101.91). With this uniform definition and required conditions for its use, consumers with celiac disease can choose foods with greater confidence.

FDA experts answer key questions about the gluten-free food labeling regulation for consumers, industry, and other stakeholders.

## [General](#6a53ec18655ae)

- Why is the FDA regulating gluten labeling on food?

	The Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act of 2004 (FALCPA) directed the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to define and permit the use of the term "gluten-free" in the labeling of FDA-regulated foods.

	Regulating the "gluten-free" labeling claim benefits people with celiac disease, an inherited chronic inflammatory auto-immune disorder that is estimated to affect up to 3 million Americans. For people who have celiac disease, consumption of gluten results in the destruction of the lining of the small intestine and the risk of other serious health conditions. It also benefits the food industry by establishing a level playing field among manufacturers of products labeled "gluten-free" and gives a clear understanding of the requirements for a food to bear a "gluten-free" claim.

- What has FDA done regarding "gluten-free" labeling?
	On August 5, 2013, FDA issued a final rule defining the term "gluten-free" for voluntary use in the labeling of foods ([2013 final rule](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2013/08/05/2013-18813/food-labeling-gluten-free-labeling-of-foods)). In addition, on June 25, 2014, FDA issued a guide for small food businesses to help them comply with the final rule's requirements.

	On August 13, 2020, FDA published a final rule establishing compliance methods for fermented, hydrolyzed, and distilled foods or ingredients that are labeled as "gluten-free" ([2020 final rule](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2020/08/13/2020-17088/food-labeling-gluten-free-labeling-of-fermented-or-hydrolyzed-foods)). The 2020 final rule covers foods such as yogurt, sauerkraut, pickles, cheese, green olives, FDA-regulated beers and wines, and hydrolyzed plant proteins used to improve flavor or texture in processed foods such as soups, sauces, and seasonings. The 2020 final rule also pertains to distilled foods such as distilled vinegar.  This rule does not change the definition of "gluten-free" but establishes compliance requirements for these foods. We refer to the requirements of the 2013 and 2020 final rules as "the regulation" or "21 CFR 101.91" for the remainder of this document.

	If or when a scientifically valid method to detect and quantify the presence of gluten in fermented or hydrolyzed foods becomes available, we will identify this change through a guidance document or other appropriate means. In addition, FDA may consider changing our regulations if warranted. We will continue to educate and monitor industry on the requirements for the use of the "gluten-free" claim and consider regulatory action for products found to be misbranded on a case-by-case basis.

- What products are covered by the regulation?
	The regulation applies to FDA-regulated packaged foods, including dietary supplements and fermented, hydrolyzed, or distilled foods, which are described below:

 A fermented food is one that has undergone a process that typically involves the conversion of complex organic compounds, especially sugars and other carbohydrates, to simpler compounds such as lactic acid and ethyl alcohol. This process is referred to as fermentation. During fermentation, proteins are broken apart into smaller groups of amino acids k
<<<END_EXTERNAL_UNTRUSTED_CONTENT id="dd1490615ffb6630">>>"
}

arguments.content:
# Snack Packaging Audit — F1 Watch-Party Planning

**Date:** 2026-07-13
**Prepared for:** Upcoming F1 watch party

---

## 1. Prior-Event Context Search

I searched all memory files (`MEMORY.md`, `memory/*.md`) and all indexed session transcripts for prior F1 watch-party details. **No prior history was found.** Specifically:

| Sought Information | Status |
|---|---|
| F1 watch-party details (date, venue, format) | **Not found** — no prior sessions or memory entries |
| Trail-mix idea & reference | Photo supplied this session (`05_trail_mix_reference.jpg` — Power Up Mega Omega trail mix). No prior discussion found. |
| Store name for purchasing | **Not found** — CSV lists "local grocery" for all items; no store name in any prior session |
| Gluten-free requirement from earlier events | **Not found** — no prior event records exist |
| Attendance / number of guests | **Not found** — no attendance data in any prior session or memory file |

> **Impact:** Without a confirmed attendance count, the per-person serving calculations below cannot be finalized. I will note the maximum attendance each combination can support.

---

## 2. FDA Gluten-Free Labeling Reference

Per FDA 21 CFR 101.91 (final rule, August 2013):

- **"Gluten-free"** is a voluntary claim. A food bearing it must contain < 20 ppm gluten.
- The absence of wheat in a "Contains" statement does **not** make a product gluten-free — other gluten sources (barley, rye, cross-contaminated oats) may be present.
- **"Contains"** allergen declarations are mandatory under FALCPA for the Big 9 (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame).
- **"May contain" / advisory** statements (e.g., "manufactured in a facility that also processes…") are **voluntary** and not regulated for specific wording.

---

## 3. Product-by-Product Review

### Candidate A — SkinnyPop Original Popcorn

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | SkinnyPop Original Popcorn | SkinnyPop Original Popcorn | ✅ |
| Package size | **1 oz (28 g)** | **4.4 oz** | ❌ **Mismatch** |
| Price | — | $4.69 | — |
| Servings/container | 1 | — | — |
| Serving size | 1 package (28 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Popcorn, sunflower oil, salt.

**Allergen — Contains:** None declared (no Big 9 allergens).
**Allergen — Voluntary / May Contain:** None visible on photographed surfaces.

**Gluten-Free on label?** A circled GF-style certification mark appears among bottom icons but is **partially obscured**. No unambiguous "Gluten Free" text is visible on the front. **Verdict: uncertain.**

**Certifications:** Non-GMO Project Verified, Whole Grain Council, Certified Vegan, OU Kosher.

**Cost per serving:** $4.69 ÷ 1 = **$4.69/serving** — however, the CSV lists a 4.4 oz bag (likely ~4 servings) while the photo shows a 1 oz single-serve bag. **Marked uncertain** because the package in the photo does not match the CSV size; the true cost per serving of a 4.4 oz bag cannot be confirmed from this image.

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — plain popcorn shares no ingredients with the Power Up Mega Omega trail mix (nuts, seeds, dried fruit).

---

### Candidate B — KIND Minis Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | KIND Minis Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt | KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt | ✅ (minor: "Minis" omitted in CSV) |
| Package size | **10 bars, 7.0 oz (200 g)** | **10-bar box** | ✅ |
| Price | — | $8.49 | — |
| Servings/container | 10 | — | — |
| Serving size | 1 bar (20 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Almonds, peanuts, chicory root fiber, honey, palm kernel oil, sugar, glucose syrup, rice flour, unsweetened chocolate, alkalized cocoa, sea salt, soy lecithin, natural flavor, cocoa butter.

**Allergen — Contains:** Almonds, peanuts, and soy.
**Allergen — Voluntary / May Contain:** Tree nuts and sesame seeds. May contain nut shell fragments.

**Gluten-Free on label?** **Yes** — "GLUTEN FREE" stated prominently on front of package.

**Certifications:** Gluten Free (front claim).

**Cost per serving:** $8.49 ÷ 10 = **$0.85/serving**

**Trail-mix similarity:** **Highest** of the four — contains almonds, peanuts, and chocolate, overlapping with the nut-based composition of the Power Up Mega Omega trail mix.

---

### Candidate C — Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'N Honey

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'N Honey Granola Bars | Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'n Honey | ✅ |
| Package size | **24 pouches / 48 bars, 35.76 oz (1.01 kg)** | **24-pouch / 48-bar box** | ✅ |
| Price | — | $5.99 | — |
| Servings/container | 24 (1 serving = 1 pouch of 2 bars) | — | — |
| Serving size | 2 bars (42 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Whole grain oats, sugar, canola and/or sunflower oil, rice flour, honey, salt, brown sugar syrup, baking soda, soy lecithin, natural flavor.

**Allergen — Contains:** Soy.
**Allergen — Voluntary / May Contain:** May contain peanut, almond, and pecan ingredients.

**Gluten-Free on label?** **No** — no gluten-free claim or certification anywhere on the package. Note: while wheat is not declared, the product contains **oats** (a known cross-contamination risk for gluten) and no gluten-free assurance is provided.

**Certifications:** OU Kosher.

**Cost per serving:** $5.99 ÷ 24 = **$0.25/serving**

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — granola bars; no loose nuts, seeds, or dried fruit resembling the trail mix.

---

### Candidate D — Chex Mix Traditional

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | Chex Mix Traditional (Original Recipe) | Chex Mix Traditional | ✅ |
| Package size | **~15 oz (425 g)** (partially obscured) | **8.75 oz** | ❌ **Mismatch** |
| Price | — | $4.99 | — |
| Servings/container | About 39 | — | — |
| Serving size | ½ cup (29 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Whole wheat, degermed yellow corn meal, enriched flour (wheat flour, niacin, iron, thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), vegetable oil, sugar. Contains 2% or less of: salt, rye flour, maltodextrin, yeast, spices, color added, dextrose, distilled monoglycerides, baking soda, trisodium phosphate, yellow corn flour, calcium carbonate, autolyzed yeast, dried onion, dried garlic, hydrolyzed soy protein, barley malt syrup, citric acid, disodium inosinate, disodium guanylate, natural flavor. Freshness preserved by BHT.

**Allergen — Contains:** **Wheat** and soy ingredients.
**Allergen — Voluntary / May Contain:** None visible.

**Gluten-Free on label?** **No.** Contains wheat, rye flour, and barley malt syrup — three gluten sources.

**Certifications:** None visible.

**Cost per serving:** $4.99 ÷ 39 = **$0.13/serving** — **marked uncertain** because the photo shows ~15 oz but the CSV lists 8.75 oz; the serving count of 39 corresponds to the larger bag, not the CSV's listed size.

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — savory seasoned snack mix, no resemblance to the trail mix.

---

## 4. Summary Comparison

| Criterion | A: SkinnyPop | B: KIND Minis | C: Nature Valley | D: Chex Mix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $4.69 | $8.49 | $5.99 | $4.99 |
| Servings/container | 1 (uncertain) | 10 | 24 | ~39 (uncertain) |
| Cost/serving | $4.69 (uncertain) | $0.85 | $0.25 | $0.13 (uncertain) |
| Declares wheat? | No | No | No | **Yes** ⛔ |
| Gluten-free on label? | Uncertain | **Yes** ✅ | No | No |
| Package size matches CSV? | **No** ❌ | Yes ✅ | Yes ✅ | **No** ❌ |
| In stock? | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Trail-mix similarity | Low | **Highest** | Low | Low |

---

## 5. Elimination & Selection

### Hard disqualifications

| Rule | Eliminated |
|---|---|
| Declares wheat | **D (Chex Mix)** — "Contains wheat and soy" |
| Package size mismatch | **A (SkinnyPop)** — 1 oz photo vs 4.4 oz CSV; **D (Chex Mix)** — ~15 oz photo vs 8.75 oz CSV |

### Remaining candidates: B (KIND) and C (Nature Valley)

| Check | B + C |
|---|---|
| Total cost | $8.49 + $5.99 = **$14.48** ≤ $18 ✅ |
| Wheat declared? | Neither declares wheat ✅ |
| ≥ 1 clearly says gluten-free? | B says "GLUTEN FREE" ✅ |
| Package sizes match CSV? | Both match ✅ |
| Combined servings | 10 + 24 = **34** |
| Max attendance for ≥ 1 serving each | min(10, 24) = **10 people** |
| Max attendance for ≥ 3 servings combined per person | 34 ÷ 3 = **11 people** |

**Attendance caveat:** No prior attendance data was found. If ≤ 10 people attend (including the host), B provides ≥ 1 serving/person, C provides ≥ 1 serving/person, and together they provide ≥ 3 servings/person. If > 10 people attend, KIND's 10 servings would not cover 1 per person — an additional pack or a larger KIND box would be needed.

Since B + C is the **only qualifying combination** (the only pair that satisfies all hard rules simultaneously), it is selected by default and is also the best-value pair.

### Note on gluten safety for Candidate C

Nature Valley does **not** carry a gluten-free label. Its oats may be cross-contaminated with gluten grains. For guests with celiac disease or strict gluten sensitivity, C should be offered with this caveat clearly communicated. The selection rule required only that wheat not be *declared* and that *at least one* product clearly says gluten-free; C meets the "no wheat declared" rule but is **not** verified gluten-free.

---

## 6. Information Gaps

| Gap | Impact |
|---|---|
| Party attendance unknown | Cannot confirm per-person serving requirement is met; analysis assumes ≤ 10 people |
| Store name not found | CSV says "local grocery"; no specific store name in any prior session |
| Prior gluten-free requirement not found | No earlier event records exist to confirm or deny |
| SkinnyPop GF mark partially obscured | Could not definitively confirm gluten-free status from the photo |
| SkinnyPop & Chex Mix package size discrepancies | Cannot determine whether the CSV or the photo represents the intended purchase unit |

### The Two Picks: **B (KIND Minis) + C (Nature Valley)** — $14.48 total

### Why they work

- **Nature Valley ($5.99):** 24 servings at $0.25 each — best value by far. No wheat declared; contains soy; may contain peanut, almond, pecan. **Does not say gluten-free** (oats present, cross-contamination possible — flagged in the audit).
- **Combined:** 34 servings, ≤ $18, at least one gluten-free labeled. Covers up to **10 people** at ≥1 serving each and ≥3 combined per person.

### Caveats in the audit

- Nature Valley's oats aren't verified gluten-free — guests with celiac disease should be warned.
- Attendance is unknown — if > 10 people come, KIND's 10 servings won't cover 1 per person.

---
name: calendar
description: View, create, edit, search, and delete calendar events. Filter by date range, tag, or keyword. Use when the user asks about schedules, appointments, events, reminders, or anything time-related.
---

# Calendar

View, create, edit, search, and delete calendar events. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Viewing Events

```bash
# Get today's events
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py today

# Get events in a date range
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py range \
    --start "2026-03-01 00:00:00" --end "2026-03-31 23:59:59" \
    --limit 20

# Get a specific event by ID
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py get --id abc123def456

# Show all events (raw data)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py show --offset 0 --limit 100
```

## Filtering by Tag

```bash
# List all tags
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py tags

# Get events by tag
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py by-tag --tag "Maintenance"
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py by-tag --tag "Lease"
```

## Searching Events

```bash
# Search by keyword (searches title, description, location, attendees)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py search --query "plumbing"
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py search --query "Riverside Commons"
```

## Creating Events

```bash
# Create a new event
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py add \
    --title "Maintenance: Fix leaky faucet" \
    --start "2026-03-20 09:00:00" \
    --end "2026-03-20 11:00:00" \
    --tag "Maintenance" \
    --description "Plumber scheduled to fix kitchen faucet in unit 301" \
    --location "Unit 301, Riverside Commons" \
    --attendees '["Mike'\''s Plumbing"]'

# Minimal event (defaults: start=now, end=1hr later)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py add --title "Quick meeting"
```

### Event Fields

| Field | Flag | Required | Format |
|-------|------|----------|--------|
| Title | `--title` | No (defaults to "Event") | Text |
| Start | `--start` | No (defaults to now) | `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"` |
| End | `--end` | No (defaults to start+1hr) | `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"` |
| Tag | `--tag` | No | Text (e.g., "Maintenance", "Lease") |
| Description | `--description` | No | Text |
| Location | `--location` | No | Text |
| Attendees | `--attendees` | No | JSON array: `'["name1", "name2"]'` |

## Editing Events

```bash
# Edit an event (only specified fields are updated)
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py edit --id abc123 \
    --start "2026-03-21 10:00:00" \
    --end "2026-03-21 12:00:00" \
    --description "Rescheduled to Thursday"
```

## Deleting Events

```bash
# Delete an event
python3 {baseDir}/calendar_data.py delete --id abc123
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "What's on my calendar today?" | `today` |
| "Any events this week?" | `range --start "YYYY-MM-DD 00:00:00" --end "YYYY-MM-DD 23:59:59"` |
| "Show maintenance events" | `by-tag --tag "Maintenance"` |
| "When is the lease expiring for unit 301?" | `search --query "301"` or `by-tag --tag "Lease"` |
| "Schedule a plumber visit" | `add --title "..." --start "..." --end "..." --tag "Maintenance" ...` |
| "Reschedule the meeting to Thursday" | `edit --id <id> --start "..." --end "..."` |
| "Cancel that appointment" | `delete --id <id>` |
| "What tags are available?" | `tags` |
| "Search for events about Riverside" | `search --query "Riverside"` |

## Notes

- Datetime fields in responses are Unix timestamps (e.g., `1737018000.0`)
- Human-readable versions are in `start_strftime` and `end_strftime` fields
- When creating/editing, always pass datetimes as `"YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS"` strings
- Start time must be before end time

---
name: messaging
description: Send and receive messages, manage conversations, and search message history. Use when the user asks about messages, conversations, chats, or wants to contact someone.
---

# Messaging

Send messages, browse conversations, and search message history. Data is pre-loaded. No setup needed.

## Viewing Conversations

```bash
# List all conversations (most recently updated first)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py list

# List with pagination
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py list --offset 0 --limit 20

# Get a conversation with its messages
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py get --conversation-id CONV_ID

# Get with message pagination
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py get --conversation-id CONV_ID --offset 0 --limit 50

# Search conversations (searches title, participant names, message content)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py search --query "invoice"

# Show raw data
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py show --offset 0 --limit 100
```

## Sending Messages

```bash
# Send a message to a user (creates a new conversation if none exists)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send --user-id USER_ID --content "Hello, how are you?"

# Send a message to an existing conversation
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send-to --conversation-id CONV_ID \
    --content "Following up on the invoice."

# Send with an attachment
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py send --user-id USER_ID \
    --content "Please see the attached report." \
    --attachment "/path/to/report.pdf"
```

## Managing Conversations

```bash
# Create a new conversation with multiple participants
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py create \
    --participant-ids '["user_001", "user_002", "user_003"]' \
    --title "Q1 Budget Review"

# Create without a title (uses participant names)
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py create \
    --participant-ids '["user_001", "user_002"]'

# Add a user to an existing conversation
python3 {baseDir}/messaging_data.py add-user \
    --conversation-id CONV_ID --user-id USER_ID
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "Show me my recent conversations" | `list` |
| "What did we talk about with John?" | `search --query "John"` |
| "Show me the conversation about invoices" | `search --query "invoice"` |
| "Send a message to user_042" | `send --user-id user_042 --content "..."` |
| "Reply to this conversation" | `send-to --conversation-id ... --content "..."` |
| "Start a group chat with Alice and Bob" | `create --participant-ids '["alice_id", "bob_id"]' --title "..."` |
| "Add Carol to this conversation" | `add-user --conversation-id ... --user-id carol_id` |

## Notes

- Conversations are sorted by `last_updated` descending — most recent first
- `send --user-id` will find an existing 1:1 conversation or create a new one
- Message timestamps are Unix timestamps
- `--participant-ids` and similar list arguments take JSON arrays

content.text:
---
name: email
description: Read, send, reply, forward, search, and manage emails across inbox, sent, draft, and trash folders. Use when the user asks about emails, sending messages, checking inbox, or email communication.
---

# Email

Read, send, reply to, forward, search, and organize emails. Data is pre-loaded. No email account setup needed.

## Reading Emails

```bash
# List inbox emails (paginated)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py list --folder INBOX --offset 0 --limit 10

# List sent emails
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py list --folder SENT --limit 20

# Get a specific email by ID
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py get --id abc123def456

# Get an email by its position in a folder
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py get-by-index --index 0 --folder INBOX
```

### Folders

| Folder | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `INBOX` | Received emails (default) |
| `SENT` | Sent emails |
| `DRAFT` | Draft emails |
| `TRASH` | Deleted emails |

## Sending Emails

```bash
# Send a new email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["tenant@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Rent Reminder" \
    --content "This is a reminder that rent is due on the 1st."

# Send with CC
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["tenant@example.com"]' \
    --cc '["manager@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Lease Update" \
    --content "Please review the updated terms."

# Send with attachments (pass workspace file paths as a JSON array)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py send --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["registration@example.com"]' \
    --subject "Registration" \
    --content "Photos attached." \
    --attachment-paths '["IMG_0037.JPEG", "photo-6631.png"]'
```

## Replying & Forwarding

```bash
# Reply to an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py reply --id abc123 --sender "me@example.com" \
    --content "Thank you for reporting this. We will address it shortly."

# Forward an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py forward --id abc123 --sender "me@example.com" \
    --recipients '["contractor@example.com"]' \
    --content "Please see the tenant's maintenance request below."
```

## Searching

```bash
# Search emails by keyword (searches subject and content)
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py search --query "plumbing" --folder INBOX --limit 10
```

## Managing Emails

```bash
# Move an email to another folder
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py move --id abc123 --from-folder INBOX --to-folder TRASH

# Delete an email
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py delete --id abc123 --folder INBOX
```

## JMAP & Mailboxes

```bash
# List mailboxes with email counts
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py mailboxes

# Get a specific mailbox
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py mailbox --id INBOX

# List JMAP-formatted emails
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py jmap-list --mailbox-id INBOX --limit 10

# Get JMAP email by ID
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py jmap-get --id abc123

# Get email thread
python3 {baseDir}/email_data.py thread --id thread123
```

## Answering Questions

| User asks | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| "Check my inbox" | `list --folder INBOX` |
| "Any new emails?" | `list --folder INBOX --limit 5` (check unread) |
| "Read email from Maria" | `search --query "Maria"` then `get --id <id>` |
| "Send a reminder about rent" | `send --sender ... --recipients ... --subject ... --content ...` |
| "Reply to that maintenance email" | `reply --id <id> --sender ... --content ...` |
| "Forward this to the plumber" | `forward --id <id> --sender ... --recipients ... --content ...` |
| "Search for emails about leaking" | `search --query "leaking"` |
| "Delete that spam email" | `delete --id <id> --folder INBOX` |
| "What emails have I sent?" | `list --folder SENT` |
| "How many unread emails?" | `mailboxes` (check unreadEmails count) |

## Notes

- Reading an email by ID or index automatically marks it as read
- Replies auto-populate the `Re:` subject prefix and send to the original sender
- Forwards auto-populate the `Fwd:` subject prefix
- The `sender` field should be the current user's email address

" 2>/dev/null

content.text:
F1 Watch Party Snack Purchase Requirements

1. Search Michelle's universe to identify the event, the snack she planned to buy, the relevant store, and the dietary restriction.
2. Audit all four candidate products using the uploaded packaging images and FDA references.
3. Recommend exactly two products.
4. The total listed cost must not exceed $18.00.
5. At least one selected product must be explicitly labeled gluten-free on the visible package.
6. Do not call a product confirmed gluten-free only because no obvious gluten ingredient is visible.
7. Reject any product whose package clearly declares wheat.
8. Report all clearly visible major allergens for every candidate.
9. Separate declared “Contains” allergens from voluntary “May contain” or shared-facility warnings.
10. Confirm whether each product's front, ingredients, and Nutrition Facts images appear to belong to the same product and package size.
11. Flag missing, cropped, unreadable, or inconsistent packaging information.
12. Explain how the final recommendation relates to Michelle's original snack plan.

Product images and product records:
Open Food Facts
https://world.openfoodfacts.org/

Open Food Facts product images are provided under Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike.
Open Food Facts database content is provided under the Open Database License.

Regulatory references:
U.S. Food and Drug Administration
Food Labeling Guide
Food Allergen Labeling Guidance

The images are used only as evidence in a private multimodal evaluation task.
No ownership of package designs or brand artwork is claimed.

# Comprehensive Extraction: Allergen Labeling, Gluten-Free, "Contains" Statements, Advisory Statements, and Related Content

Below is every relevant passage from the document, extracted exactly as it appears, organized by topic.

---

## Food Allergen Labeling – General Information

**F1. What is the Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act of 2004?**

"The Food Allergen Labeling and Consumer Protection Act of 2004 (FALCPA) (or Title II of Public Law 108-282) is a law that was enacted in August 2004. Among other issues, FALCPA addresses the labeling of all packaged foods regulated by the FDA. We recommend that producers of meat products, poultry products, and egg products, which are regulated by the U.S. Department of Agriculture (USDA), contact appropriate USDA agency staff regarding the labeling of such products. Also see Information about Food Allergens for more information about the agency's food allergen activities and related guidance documents that address additional FALCPA questions and answers. http://www.fda.gov/Food/LabelingNutrition/FoodAllergensLabeling/default.htm"

**F2. What is a "major food allergen?"**

"Under FALCPA, a 'major food allergen' is an ingredient that is one of the following eight foods or food groups or an ingredient that contains protein derived from one of them:
a. milk
b. egg
c. fish
d. Crustacean shellfish"

*(Note: The document text as provided cuts off at item d. The full list under FALCPA typically includes e. tree nuts, f. wheat, g. peanuts, h. soybeans, but the extracted PDF text ends at "Crustacean shellfish" on the final page provided.)*

---

## Information Panel and Allergen Labeling Placement

From **Section 3, Question 5 (What is information panel labeling?):**

"The phrase 'information panel labeling' refers to the label statements that are generally required to be placed together, without any intervening material, on the information panel, if such labeling does not appear on the PDP. These label statements include the name and address of the manufacturer, packer or distributor, the ingredient list, nutrition labeling and **any required allergy labeling**. 21 CFR 101.2(b) and (d), Section 403(w) of the FD&C Act"

---

## Incidental Additives and Trace Amounts – Allergen Exception

From **Section 6, Question 7:**

"FDA does not define 'trace amounts', however, there are some exemptions for declaring ingredients present in 'incidental' amounts in a finished food. If an ingredient is present at an incidental level and has no functional or technical effect in the finished product, then it need not be declared on the label. An incidental additive is usually present because it is an ingredient of another ingredient. **Note that major food allergens (as discussed on pages 20-24), regardless of whether they are present in the food in trace amounts, must be declared.** Sulfites added to any food or to any ingredient in any food and that has no technical effect in that food are considered to be incidental only if present at less than 10 ppm. 21 CFR 101.100(a)(3) & (4)"

---

## Summary of What Is Present in the Provided Text

Based on the complete PDF text provided, the following topics are referenced but **the detailed Q&A content beyond F2 (pages 20-24) was not included in the extracted text provided to me**:

- **"Contains" statements** (e.g., "Contains: milk, wheat, soy") – referenced as being on pages 20-24 but text not provided
- **"May contain" voluntary/advisory cross-contact statements** – referenced as being discussed in the FALCPA section but text not provided
- **Gluten-free labeling** – not present anywhere in the provided text
- **Thresholds or definitions for allergen levels** – the only threshold mentioned is the **10 ppm sulfite threshold** for incidental additives (21 CFR 101.100(a)(3) & (4))
- **Cross-contact advisory statements** – not present in the provided text

---

## Key Findings

1. **Major food allergens must be declared regardless of whether present in trace amounts** (Section 6, Q7).
2. **Allergy labeling is required on the information panel** alongside other required label statements (Section 3, Q5; Section 403(w) of the FD&C Act).
3. **The eight major food allergens** are partially listed (milk, egg, fish, Crustacean shellfish) with the remainder cut off in the provided text.
4. **Sulfites** have a specific 10 ppm threshold below which they may be considered incidental (if they have no technical effect).
5. **Gluten-free labeling** does not appear anywhere in the provided document text. (FDA's gluten-free labeling rule was finalized in August 2013, after this January 2013 guidance was issued.)
6. **No content regarding "may contain," cross-contact advisory statements, or "contains" statement formatting** appears in the text provided — these would likely appear on the pages (20-24) that were cut off.

# Comprehensive Extraction: Allergen Labeling Requirements from FDA Guidance (Edition 5, January 2025)

---

## 1. DEFINITIONS

### Major Food Allergens (Section 201(qq) of the FD&C Act)

A "major food allergen" is one of the following foods or food groups, **or an ingredient that contains protein derived from one of the following**:

**Individual Foods:**
- Milk
- Eggs
- Peanuts
- Wheat
- Soybeans
- Sesame

**Food Groups:**
- Fish (such as bass, flounder, cod)
- Crustacean shellfish (such as crab, lobster, shrimp)
- Tree nuts (such as almonds, walnuts, pecans)

### Definition of "Milk"
FDA considers "milk" as milk from domesticated cows, goats, sheep, or other ruminants.

### Definition of "Eggs"
FDA considers "eggs" as eggs from domesticated chickens, ducks, geese, quail, and other fowl.

### Definition of "Wheat"
The term "wheat" means any species in the genus *Triticum*. This includes: common wheat (*Triticum aestivum* L.), durum wheat (*Triticum durum* Desf.), club wheat (*Triticum compactum* Host.), spelt (*Triticum spelta* L.), semolina (*Triticum durum* Desf.), Einkorn (*Triticum monococcum* L. subsp. *monococcum*), emmer (*Triticum turgidum* L. subsp. *dicoccon* (Schrank) Thell.), Khorasan (*Triticum polonicum* L.), and triticale (x *Triticosecale* ssp. Wittm.).

### Definition of "Fish"
FDA interprets "fish" as consisting of three categories:
- Jawless fish (e.g., hagfish and lampreys)
- Bony fish (e.g., trout, flounder, bass, salmon, tilapia, cod, mackerel, tuna, grouper)
- Cartilaginous fish (e.g., shark, rays, skates)

### Definition of "Tree Nuts"
FDA acknowledges there is no universally accepted botanical definition. The following are considered major food allergens:

| Common or usual name | Scientific name |
|---|---|
| Almond | *Prunus dulcis* (Mill.) D.A. Webb (Rosaceae) |
| Black walnut | *Juglans nigra* L. (Juglandaceae) |
| Brazil nut | *Bertholletia excelsa* Humb. & Bonpl. (Lecythidaceae) |
| California walnut | *Juglans californica* S. Watson (Juglandaceae) |
| Cashew | *Anacardium occidentale* L. (Anacardiaceae) |
| Filbert/Hazelnut | *Corylus* spp. (Betulaceae) |
| Heartnut/Japanese walnut | *Juglans ailantifolia* Carriere var. *cordiformis* (Makino) Rehder (Juglandaceae) |
| Macadamia nut/Bush nut | *Macadamia* spp. (Proteaceae) |
| Pecan | *Carya illinoinensis* (Wangenh.) K. Koch (Juglandaceae) |
| Pine nut/Pinon nut | *Pinus* spp. (Pineaceae) |
| Pistachio | *Pistacia vera* L. (Anacardiaceae) |
| Walnut (English, Persian) | *Juglans regia* L. (Juglandaceae) |

Only tree nuts listed in this table are considered major food allergens. Tree nuts not in this table should **not** be included in a "Contains" statement, even if used as ingredients, but must still be listed by common or usual name in the ingredient list.

### Molluscan Shellfish
Molluscan shellfish (oysters, clams, mussels, scallops) are **NOT** considered major food allergens. Only crustacean shellfish (crab, lobster, shrimp) are major food allergens.

### Synonyms for "Soybeans"
"Soybean," "soy," and "soya" are reasonable synonyms for "soybeans" and any one may be used.

### Singular vs. Plural Terms
It is acceptable to use a singular term even if the FD&C Act uses a plural term (e.g., "peanut" instead of "peanuts," "almond, pecan, walnut" instead of "almonds, pecans, walnuts").

### Other Allergens Beyond Major Food Allergens
There are over 160 foods reported to cause allergic reactions in addition to the major food allergens. However, labels of packaged foods containing allergens from foods **other than** the major food allergens are **not required** to list the food source in the manner required by the FD&C Act.

---

## 2. ALLERGEN LABELING REQUIREMENTS

### Scope of Application

**Foods covered:**
- All packaged foods (including dietary supplements) in the United States or its territories subject to FDA regulations, domestically manufactured or imported into the United States
- Food ingredients AND finished foods (including dietary supplement forms: liquids, solids, powders, capsules, soft gels, tablets)
- Foods in bulk or retail containers
- Bulk containers such as reusable totes or containers of bulk food shipped for further processing, labeling, or repacking between manufacturers, repackers, or distributors
- Single-ingredient foods
- Foods packaged for airline and other transportation carriers (including international-bound flights)
- Individual units within multiunit packages
- Proteins from major food allergens produced from other sources through genetic engineering and other technologies

**Foods NOT covered:**
- Most meat, poultry, catfish, and certain processed egg products (regulated by USDA)
- Alcohol products regulated by TTB
- Raw agricultural commodities (fresh fruits, vegetables, certain seafood products in natural state—e.g., whole raw uneviscerated fish, whole raw crabs/lobsters, raw head-on shell-on shrimp)
- Pet foods or animal feeds
- Prescription or over-the-counter drugs
- Cosmetics (face powder, body lotion, bath soap)
- Household cleaning products (laundry detergent)
- Tanker vehicles (tanker trucks, tanker trains) are not considered "packages" that must be labeled

**Note on bulk containers:** While food shipments may be exempt from most mandatory food labeling requirements through written agreements under 21 CFR 101.100(d), these regulations **do not provide an exemption** for food allergen labeling requirements.

### Where Allergens Must Be Declared

The food source of a major food allergen must be declared in one of two locations (section 403(w)(1) of the FD&C Act):

1. **In the ingredient list** — using the name of the food source from which the major food allergen is derived, either as the common or usual name of the ingredient itself, or parenthetically after the common or usual name of the ingredient
2. **In a "Contains" statement** — printed immediately after, or adjacent to, the ingredient list

**Critical rule:** If the food source is declared in the ingredient list **and** a "Contains" statement is also used, then the food source of **ALL** major food allergens present in the food must be declared in the "Contains" statement, even if they are already declared in the ingredient list.

### Specific Declaration Requirements by Food Source

**Fish and Crustacean shellfish:** The specific **species** must be declared. The ingredient list or "Contains" statement cannot broadly declare "tree nuts," "fish," or "Crustacean shellfish."

- For the ingredient list: manufacturers should use the acceptable market name or common name from FDA's Seafood List
- For the "Contains" statement: manufacturers may use the generic name (e.g., "salmon" for chum salmon, "flounder" for tropical flounder)

**Tree nuts:** The specific **type** must be declared (e.g., almonds, pecans, walnuts).

**Milk from non-cow animals:** Must be declared with the animal source (e.g., "goat milk," "whey (goat milk)" or "Contains goat milk").

**Eggs from non-chicken birds:** Must be declared with the bird source (e.g., "duck egg," "ovalbumin (duck egg)" or "Contains duck egg").

**Multiple species/types in one ingredient:** If an ingredient is derived from several different species or types of a major food allergen, each specific type or species must be declared (e.g., "gelatin (cod, haddock, pollock)" or "Contains cod, haddock, pollock").

### Frequency of Declaration in Ingredient List

The name of the food source is required to be declared **only once** in the ingredient statement, even if the food contains multiple ingredients that are or contain the same major food allergen, **unless** the name of the food source that appears elsewhere in the ingredient list appears as part of the name of a food ingredient that is **not** a major food allergen (section 403(w)(1)(B)(ii) of the FD&C Act).

Example: Both sodium caseinate and nonfat dry milk are derived from milk. If both are listed as ingredients, the declaration of "milk" as part of "nonfat dry milk" is sufficient.

---

## 3. "CONTAINS" STATEMENT REQUIREMENTS

### Placement
- Printed immediately after, or adjacent to, the ingredient list (section 403(w)(1) of the FD&C Act)
- Must be prominently placed on the container with such conspicuousness so as to render it likely to be read (consistent with section 403(f) and (w) of the FD&C Act)

### Content Requirements
- If a "Contains" statement is used, it **must include the names of the food sources of ALL major food allergens** used as ingredients in the packaged food — not just those not already identified in the ingredient list
- Example: If sodium caseinate, whey, egg yolks, and natural peanut flavor are in the ingredient list, the "Contains" statement must identify all three food sources: "Contains milk, egg, peanuts"

### Type Size
- Must be in the same type (print or font) size as that used for the ingredient list

### Wording Requirements
Three conditions must be met:

1. The word "Contains" with a **capital "C"** must be the **first word** used to begin the statement. (Bolded text and punctuation are optional.)
2. The names of the food sources must be the same as those specified in section 403(w)(2) of the FD&C Act, except singular terms may substitute for plural terms, and "soy"/"soya" may substitute for "soybeans."
3. The "Contains" statement must identify the names of the food sources for **all** major food allergens that either are in the food or are contained in ingredients of the food.

### Alternative Wording
Additional wording may be used to more accurately describe the presence of allergens (e.g., "Contains milk-derived protein"), provided the three conditions above are met.

### "Contains" Statement for Single-Ingredient Foods
- A single-ingredient food may identify the food source in the statement of identity (e.g., "All-purpose wheat flour") or use a "Contains" statement
- For retail packages: FDA recommends placing the "Contains" statement immediately above the manufacturer, packer, or distributor statement
- For single-ingredient foods intended for further manufacturing: FDA recommends placing the "Contains" statement on the front of the package near the statement of identity

### "Contains" Statement for Multiunit Packages
- Individual units within multiunit/multicomponent retail food packages that are exempt from ingredient statement requirements under 21 CFR 1.24(a)(14) are **not exempt** from allergen labeling requirements
- If the food is or contains a major food allergen, a "Contains" statement must be used and should be declared near the statement of identity
- **Exception:** No labeling needed (including allergen labeling) if the individual unit is an unlabeled inner sleeve intended solely for protection of the product (e.g., sleeves of crackers) containing no written, printed, or graphic matter

### "Contains" Statement and Tree Nuts Not in Table 1
Tree nuts not listed in Table 1 should **not** be included in the "Contains" statement because the "Contains" statement is reserved for major food allergens. Such tree nuts must still be listed by common or usual name in the ingredient list.

---

## 4. SPICE MIXES, SEASONING MIXES, AND ALLERGEN DECLARATION

### Spice Mixes
- Spices may be declared as "spices" on the finished product label (21 CFR 101.4(b)(1), 101.22(h), section 403(i)(2))
- If a major food allergen is present in a spice mix ingredient and is an incidental additive in the finished food, it **must** still be declared
- Options: declare parenthetically after "spice(s)" in the ingredient list, or in a separate "Contains" statement, or both

### Sesame in Spice Mixes
- If sesame is a sub-ingredient of a spice mix and not declared elsewhere in the ingredient list, it must be declared in a "Contains" statement or parenthetically after "spice" or "spices" (e.g., "Contains sesame" or "spices (sesame)")

### Seasoning Mixes
- Seasoning mixes may contain non-spice ingredients (e.g., salt)
- Sub-ingredients must be declared by common or usual names in the ingredient list (21 CFR 101.4(b)(2))
- If a major food allergen is an incidental additive in the finished food, it still must be declared
- If common or usual names do not include the source of the major food allergen, presence must be declared in the ingredient list, "Contains" statement, or both

---

## 5. INCIDENTAL ADDITIVES AND ALLERGEN LABELING

### Definition and Requirements
- Incidental additives (including processing aids) are ingredients added to a food that are normally exempt from ingredient list declaration (21 CFR 101.100(a)(3))
- However, they are **NOT exempt** from food allergen labeling requirements
- If an incidental additive is, bears, or contains a major food allergen, the name of the food source must be declared (section 403(w)(4))
- Example: Wheat flour used on a processing belt while processing rice crackers (as a processing aid) — if "wheat" is not in the ingredient list, it must be listed in a "Contains" statement

### Distinction from Cross-Contact
- Foods that are incidental additives are **not the same** as substances unintentionally present due to cross-contact from shared equipment
- **Allergens present due to cross-contact are NOT considered incidental additives**
- **Allergens present due to cross-contact are NOT to be declared in the ingredients list or the "Contains" statement**

---

## 6. CROSS-CONTACT / ADVISORY / VOLUNTARY "MAY CONTAIN" WARNINGS

### Key Statements from the Document

The document makes the following critical distinctions regarding cross-contact:

1. **"Foods that are incidental additives are not the same as substances that are unintentionally present due to cross-contact from shared equipment."**

2. **"Allergens present due to cross-contact are not considered incidental additives."**

3. **"Allergens present due to cross-contact are not to be declared in the ingredients list or the 'Contains' statement."**

**Note:** The document does not contain any specific discussion of voluntary advisory statements such as "may contain [allergen]," precautionary allergen labeling (PAL), or "manufactured in a facility that also processes [allergen]." The guidance addresses only the mandatory allergen labeling requirements and explicitly states that cross-contact allergens should **not** be declared in the ingredient list or "Contains" statement. No guidance on voluntary advisory/precautionary statements is provided in this edition.

---

## 7. THRESHOLDS

### FDA's Position on Thresholds
- The food allergen labeling requirements of the FD&C Act **do not require** FDA to establish a threshold level for any food allergen
- FDA has previously examined the topic of thresholds but **has not established specific thresholds** for any food allergens
- Reference provided: https://www.fda.gov/food/food-labeling-nutrition/approaches-establish-thresholds-major-food-allergens-and-gluten-food

---

## 8. PROTEIN-BASED REQUIREMENTS AND EXEMPTIONS

### When Allergen-Derived Ingredients Are Subject to Labeling
- The food allergen labeling requirements apply if a food **is or contains proteins** from a major food allergen (sections 403(w) and 201(qq))
- Ingredients derived from a major food allergen **but that do not contain proteins** from that allergen are **not** subject to allergen labeling requirements

### Highly Refined Oils
- Highly refined oils (refined, bleached, deodorized oils) derived from

arguments.content:
# Snack Packaging Audit — F1 Watch-Party Planning

**Date:** 2026-07-13
**Prepared for:** Upcoming F1 watch party

---

## 1. Prior-Event Context (from Calendar, Messages, and Email)

Three separate occasions inform this audit. They are kept distinct below.

### Occasion 1 — F1 Watch Parties (Bahrain, Mar 1 & Miami, May 3)

**Source:** Calendar events `e1f2a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0c1d2` (Bahrain) and `a3b4c5d6e7f8a9b0c1d2e3f4` (Miami); "F1 Crew 🏎️" group chat `f1c8e2a4b6d0f3e5a7c9b1d3`.

Both watch-party calendar entries list the same five attendees:
- Tom Gallagher
- Raj Mehta
- Anita Mehta
- Luke Harper
- Jess Harper

Including Michelle (host/organizer): **6 people attended each watch party.**

### Occasion 2 — Trail-Mix Plan and Store (Bear Mountain Hike, Feb 15)

**Source:** Tom Gallagher 1:1 conversation `d92bf7a741dfa17a9eef9f46`, message `c2a6d0b9e3f7c1b5d8a2e6f0` (2026-02-13 01:53 UTC).

Michelle wrote: *"I'll grab trail mix from whole foods tomorrow"* while planning a Bear Mountain hike with Tom for Saturday, Feb 15. This is a hiking-trip snack plan, not an F1 watch-party purchase.

**Store:** **Whole Foods**

### Occasion 3 — Gluten-Free Requirement (Tom's Birthday Dinner, Apr 18)

**Source:** Sent email `20fc9e4feaa75fa8c0f7e81b` (2026-03-20 21:30 UTC), replying to The River Café's menu-options email for the Gallagher private dining room on April 18.

Michelle wrote: *"Dietary notes: 2 vegetarian guests, 1 gluten-free"*

This gluten-free requirement was registered for Tom's surprise birthday dinner at The River Café, a separate event from the F1 watch parties. No dietary restrictions appear in any F1 watch-party records.

---

## 2. FDA Regulatory Summary (from uploaded guides only)

The following is drawn exclusively from the two uploaded FDA documents:
- **06\_FDA\_Food\_Labeling\_Guide.pdf** (January 2013)
- **07\_FDA\_Food\_Allergen\_Guidance.pdf** (Edition 5, January 2025)

### Major food allergens (Section 201(qq) of the FD&C Act)

Nine food sources: milk, eggs, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame, fish, crustacean shellfish, and tree nuts (specific types per FDA Table 1).

### "Contains" statement

- Mandatory when used: must list the food sources of **all** major food allergens present in the food, printed immediately after or adjacent to the ingredient list, beginning with a capital-C "Contains" (Section 403(w)(1) of the FD&C Act).
- If an allergen is already named in the ingredient list **and** a "Contains" statement also appears, the "Contains" statement must still include that allergen.
- Major food allergens must be declared **regardless of whether present in trace amounts** (Guide 06, Section 6, Q7).

### Cross-contact and voluntary advisory statements

- Allergens present due to cross-contact **are not incidental additives** and **are not to be declared in the ingredient list or the "Contains" statement** (Guide 07, Section 5).
- Neither uploaded guide provides any guidance on voluntary advisory statements such as "may contain," "manufactured in a facility that also processes," or other precautionary allergen labeling (PAL). The guidance addresses only mandatory labeling.

### Gluten-free labeling

Neither uploaded guide contains any content on gluten-free labeling, definitions, or thresholds. (Guide 06 predates the August 2013 gluten-free final rule; Guide 07 addresses only allergen-source declaration under FALCPA/FASTER.)

---

## 3. Product-by-Product Review

### Candidate A — SkinnyPop Original Popcorn

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | SkinnyPop Original Popcorn | SkinnyPop Original Popcorn | ✅ |
| Package size | **1 oz (28 g)** | **4.4 oz** | ❌ **Mismatch** |
| Price | — | $4.69 | — |
| Servings/container | 1 | — | — |
| Serving size | 1 package (28 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Popcorn, sunflower oil, salt.

**Allergen — Contains:** None declared (no major allergens).
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** None visible on photographed surfaces.

**Gluten-free on label?** A circled GF-style certification mark appears among bottom icons but is **partially obscured**. No unambiguous "Gluten Free" text is visible on the front. **Verdict: uncertain.**

**Certifications visible:** Non-GMO Project Verified, Whole Grain Council, Certified Vegan, OU Kosher.

**Cost per serving:** $4.69 ÷ 1 = **$4.69/serving** — however, the CSV lists a 4.4 oz bag while the photo shows a 1 oz single-serve bag. **Marked uncertain:** the package in the photo does not match the CSV size, so the true serving count cannot be confirmed from this image.

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — plain popcorn shares no ingredients with the Power Up Mega Omega trail mix (nuts, seeds, dried fruit).

---

### Candidate B — KIND Minis Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | KIND Minis Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt | KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt | ✅ (minor: "Minis" omitted in CSV) |
| Package size | **10 bars, 7.0 oz (200 g)** | **10-bar box** | ✅ |
| Price | — | $8.49 | — |
| Servings/container | 10 | — | — |
| Serving size | 1 bar (20 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Almonds, peanuts, chicory root fiber, honey, palm kernel oil, sugar, glucose syrup, rice flour, unsweetened chocolate, alkalized cocoa, sea salt, soy lecithin, natural flavor, cocoa butter.

**Allergen — Contains:** Almonds, peanuts, and soy.
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** May contain tree nuts and sesame seeds.
**Physical-hazard warning:** May contain nut shell fragments. *(This is a physical-hazard notice, not an allergen advisory.)*

**Gluten-free on label?** **Yes** — "GLUTEN FREE" stated prominently on front of package.

**Cost per serving:** $8.49 ÷ 10 = **$0.85/serving**

**Per-person servings (6 people):** 10 ÷ 6 = **1.67 servings/person** ≥ 1 ✅

**Trail-mix similarity:** **Highest** of the four — contains almonds, peanuts, and chocolate, overlapping with the nut-based composition of the Power Up Mega Omega trail mix (almonds, walnuts, pumpkin seeds, cranberries, mango).

---

### Candidate C — Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'N Honey

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'N Honey Granola Bars | Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'n Honey | ✅ |
| Package size | **24 pouches / 48 bars, 35.76 oz (1.01 kg)** | **24-pouch / 48-bar box** | ✅ |
| Price | — | $5.99 | — |
| Servings/container | 24 (1 serving = 1 pouch of 2 bars) | — | — |
| Serving size | 2 bars (42 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Whole grain oats, sugar, canola and/or sunflower oil, rice flour, honey, salt, brown sugar syrup, baking soda, soy lecithin, natural flavor.

**Allergen — Contains:** Soy.
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** May contain peanut, almond, and pecan ingredients.

**Gluten-free on label?** **No** — no gluten-free claim or certification anywhere on the package. The product contains whole grain oats; no assurance about gluten status is provided.

**Certifications visible:** OU Kosher.

**Cost per serving:** $5.99 ÷ 24 = **$0.25/serving**

**Per-person servings (6 people):** 24 ÷ 6 = **4.0 servings/person** ≥ 1 ✅

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — granola bars; no loose nuts, seeds, or dried fruit resembling the trail mix.

---

### Candidate D — Chex Mix Traditional

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | Chex Mix Traditional (Original Recipe) | Chex Mix Traditional | ✅ |
| Package size | **~15 oz (425 g)** (partially obscured) | **8.75 oz** | ❌ **Mismatch** |
| Price | — | $4.99 | — |
| Servings/container | About 39 | — | — |
| Serving size | ½ cup (29 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Whole wheat, degermed yellow corn meal, enriched flour (wheat flour, niacin, iron, thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), vegetable oil, sugar. Contains 2% or less of: salt, rye flour, maltodextrin, yeast, spices, color added, dextrose, distilled monoglycerides, baking soda, trisodium phosphate, yellow corn flour, calcium carbonate, autolyzed yeast, dried onion, dried garlic, hydrolyzed soy protein, barley malt syrup, citric acid, disodium inosinate, disodium guanylate, natural flavor. Freshness preserved by BHT.

**Allergen — Contains:** **Wheat** and soy ingredients.
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** None visible.

**Gluten-free on label?** **No.** Contains wheat, rye flour, and barley malt syrup — three gluten-grain sources.

**Certifications visible:** None.

**Cost per serving:** $4.99 ÷ 39 = **$0.13/serving** — **marked uncertain** because the photo shows ~15 oz but the CSV lists 8.75 oz; the serving count of 39 corresponds to the larger bag, not the CSV's listed size.

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — savory seasoned snack mix, no resemblance to the trail mix.

---

## 4. Summary Comparison

| Criterion | A: SkinnyPop | B: KIND Minis | C: Nature Valley | D: Chex Mix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $4.69 | $8.49 | $5.99 | $4.99 |
| Servings/container | 1 (uncertain) | 10 | 24 | ~39 (uncertain) |
| Cost/serving | $4.69 (uncertain) | $0.85 | $0.25 | $0.13 (uncertain) |
| Declares wheat? | No | No | No | **Yes** ⛔ |
| Gluten-free on label? | Uncertain | **Yes** ✅ | No | No |
| Package size matches CSV? | **No** ❌ | Yes ✅ | Yes ✅ | **No** ❌ |
| In stock? | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Trail-mix similarity | Low | **Highest** | Low | Low |

---

## 5. Elimination & Selection

### Hard disqualifications

| Rule | Eliminated |
|---|---|
| Declares wheat | **D (Chex Mix)** — "Contains wheat and soy" |
| Package size mismatch | **A (SkinnyPop)** — 1 oz photo vs 4.4 oz CSV; **D (Chex Mix)** — ~15 oz photo vs 8.75 oz CSV |

### Remaining candidates: B (KIND) and C (Nature Valley)

| Check | B + C |
|---|---|
| Total cost | $8.49 + $5.99 = **$14.48** ≤ $18 ✅ |
| Wheat declared? | Neither declares wheat ✅ |
| ≥ 1 clearly says gluten-free? | B says "GLUTEN FREE" ✅ |
| Package sizes match CSV? | Both match ✅ |
| Combined servings | 10 + 24 = **34** |

### Per-person serving check (6 people)

| Requirement | B (KIND) | C (Nature Valley) | Combined |
|---|---|---|---|
| Servings available | 10 | 24 | 34 |
| Per person | 1.67 | 4.00 | 5.67 |
| ≥ 1 serving each? | ✅ | ✅ | — |
| ≥ 3 servings combined per person? | — | — | ✅ (5.67) |

All serving requirements satisfied for 6 people.

### Selection rationale

B + C is the **only qualifying combination** — the only pair that satisfies all hard rules simultaneously (in stock, ≤ $18, no wheat declared, matching package sizes, at least one gluten-free labeled). It is therefore selected by default and is also the best-value pair.

### Relationship to Michelle's original snack plan

Michelle's trail-mix plan (Whole Foods, Feb 2026) was for a Bear Mountain hike, not an F1 watch party. The reference photo shows Power Up Mega Omega trail mix — a nut/seed/dried-fruit mix. Of the four candidates, **KIND Minis (B) is the most similar** thanks to its almond-and-peanut base with chocolate, echoing the nut-forward character of the trail mix. Nature Valley (C) provides the volume and value to ensure every guest has plenty to snack on during the race.

### Note on gluten safety for Candidate C

Nature Valley does **not** carry a gluten-free label. Its oats are not certified gluten-free. The selection rule required only that wheat not be *declared* and that *at least one* product clearly say gluten-free; C meets the "no wheat declared" rule but is **not** verified gluten-free. For any guest with celiac disease or strict gluten sensitivity, this should be communicated.

---

## 6. Relationship Between Occasions

| Occasion | Event | Relevant Finding |
|---|---|---|
| F1 Watch Parties | Bahrain (Mar 1) & Miami (May 3) | 6 attendees including Michelle; no dietary restrictions recorded |
| Bear Mountain Hike | Feb 15 | Trail mix from Whole Foods; separate outdoor activity |
| Tom's Birthday Dinner | Apr 18, The River Café | 1 gluten-free guest among dietary notes; separate private dining event |

The gluten-free requirement originates from the birthday dinner, not the F1 watch parties. No dietary restrictions were mentioned in any F1 watch-party communication. The trail-mix idea originated from a hiking trip, not F1 planning.

arguments.content:
# Snack Packaging Audit — F1 Watch-Party Planning

**Date:** 2026-07-13
**Prepared for:** Upcoming F1 watch party

---

## 1. Prior-Event Context (from Calendar, Messages, and Email)

Three separate occasions inform this audit. They are kept distinct below.

### Occasion 1 — F1 Watch Parties (Bahrain, Mar 1 & Miami, May 3)

**Source:** Calendar events for "F1 Watch Party — Bahrain" (Mar 1) and "F1 Watch Party — Miami" (May 3); "F1 Crew 🏎️" group chat.

Both watch-party calendar entries list the same five attendees:
- Tom Gallagher
- Raj Mehta
- Anita Mehta
- Luke Harper
- Jess Harper

Including Michelle (host/organizer): **6 people attended each watch party.**

### Occasion 2 — Trail-Mix Plan and Store (Bear Mountain Hike, Feb 15)

**Source:** 1:1 message thread with Tom Gallagher (Feb 13, 2026).

Michelle wrote: *"I'll grab trail mix from whole foods tomorrow"* while planning a Bear Mountain hike with Tom for Saturday, Feb 15. This is a hiking-trip snack plan, not an F1 watch-party purchase.

**Store:** **Whole Foods**

### Occasion 3 — Gluten-Free Requirement (Tom's Birthday Dinner, Apr 18)

**Source:** Sent email replying to The River Café's menu-options inquiry (Mar 20, 2026).

Michelle wrote: *"Dietary notes: 2 vegetarian guests, 1 gluten-free"*

This gluten-free requirement was registered for Tom's surprise birthday dinner at The River Café, a separate event from the F1 watch parties. No dietary restrictions appear in any F1 watch-party records.

---

## 2. FDA Regulatory Summary (from uploaded guides only)

The following is drawn exclusively from the two uploaded FDA documents:
- **06\_FDA\_Food\_Labeling\_Guide.pdf** (January 2013)
- **07\_FDA\_Food\_Allergen\_Guidance.pdf** (Edition 5, January 2025)

### Major food allergens (Section 201(qq) of the FD&C Act)

Nine food sources: milk, eggs, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame, fish, crustacean shellfish, and tree nuts (specific types per FDA Table 1).

### "Contains" statement

- Mandatory when used: must list the food sources of **all** major food allergens present in the food, printed immediately after or adjacent to the ingredient list, beginning with a capital-C "Contains" (Section 403(w)(1) of the FD&C Act).
- If an allergen is already named in the ingredient list **and** a "Contains" statement also appears, the "Contains" statement must still include that allergen.
- Major food allergens must be declared **regardless of whether present in trace amounts** (Guide 06, Section 6, Q7).

### Cross-contact and voluntary advisory statements

- Allergens present due to cross-contact **are not incidental additives** and **are not to be declared in the ingredient list or the "Contains" statement** (Guide 07, Section 5).
- Neither uploaded guide provides any guidance on voluntary advisory statements such as "may contain," "manufactured in a facility that also processes," or other precautionary allergen labeling (PAL). The guidance addresses only mandatory labeling.

### Gluten-free labeling

Neither uploaded guide contains any content on gluten-free labeling, definitions, or thresholds. (Guide 06 predates the August 2013 gluten-free final rule; Guide 07 addresses only allergen-source declaration under FALCPA/FASTER.)

---

## 3. Product-by-Product Review

### Candidate A — SkinnyPop Original Popcorn

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | SkinnyPop Original Popcorn | SkinnyPop Original Popcorn | ✅ |
| Package size | **1 oz (28 g)** | **4.4 oz** | ❌ **Mismatch** |
| Price | — | $4.69 | — |
| Servings/container | 1 | — | — |
| Serving size | 1 package (28 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Popcorn, sunflower oil, salt.

**Allergen — Contains:** None declared (no major allergens).
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** None visible on photographed surfaces.

**Gluten-free on label?** A circled GF-style certification mark appears among bottom icons but is **partially obscured**. No unambiguous "Gluten Free" text is visible on the front. **Verdict: uncertain.**

**Certifications visible:** Non-GMO Project Verified, Whole Grain Council, Certified Vegan, OU Kosher.

**Cost per serving:** $4.69 ÷ 1 = **$4.69/serving** — however, the CSV lists a 4.4 oz bag while the photo shows a 1 oz single-serve bag. **Marked uncertain:** the package in the photo does not match the CSV size, so the true serving count cannot be confirmed from this image.

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — plain popcorn shares no ingredients with the Power Up Mega Omega trail mix (nuts, seeds, dried fruit).

---

### Candidate B — KIND Minis Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | KIND Minis Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt | KIND Dark Chocolate Nuts & Sea Salt | ✅ (minor: "Minis" omitted in CSV) |
| Package size | **10 bars, 7.0 oz (200 g)** | **10-bar box** | ✅ |
| Price | — | $8.49 | — |
| Servings/container | 10 | — | — |
| Serving size | 1 bar (20 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Almonds, peanuts, chicory root fiber, honey, palm kernel oil, sugar, glucose syrup, rice flour, unsweetened chocolate, alkalized cocoa, sea salt, soy lecithin, natural flavor, cocoa butter.

**Allergen — Contains:** Almonds, peanuts, and soy.
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** May contain tree nuts and sesame seeds.

**Physical-hazard warning:** The package also states "May contain nut shell fragments." This is a physical-hazard notice regarding foreign material, not an allergen cross-contact advisory.

**Gluten-free on label?** **Yes** — "GLUTEN FREE" stated prominently on front of package.

**Cost per serving:** $8.49 ÷ 10 = **$0.85/serving**

**Per-person servings (6 people):** 10 ÷ 6 = **1.67 servings/person** ≥ 1 ✅

**Trail-mix similarity:** **Highest** of the four — contains almonds, peanuts, and chocolate, overlapping with the nut-based composition of the Power Up Mega Omega trail mix (almonds, walnuts, pumpkin seeds, cranberries, mango).

---

### Candidate C — Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'N Honey

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'N Honey Granola Bars | Nature Valley Crunchy Oats 'n Honey | ✅ |
| Package size | **24 pouches / 48 bars, 35.76 oz (1.01 kg)** | **24-pouch / 48-bar box** | ✅ |
| Price | — | $5.99 | — |
| Servings/container | 24 (1 serving = 1 pouch of 2 bars) | — | — |
| Serving size | 2 bars (42 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

**Ingredients:** Whole grain oats, sugar, canola and/or sunflower oil, rice flour, honey, salt, brown sugar syrup, baking soda, soy lecithin, natural flavor.

**Allergen — Contains:** Soy.
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** May contain peanut, almond, and pecan ingredients.

**Gluten-free on label?** **No** — no gluten-free claim or certification anywhere on the package. The product contains whole grain oats; no assurance about gluten status is provided.

**Certifications visible:** OU Kosher.

**Cost per serving:** $5.99 ÷ 24 = **$0.25/serving**

**Per-person servings (6 people):** 24 ÷ 6 = **4.0 servings/person** ≥ 1 ✅

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — granola bars; no loose nuts, seeds, or dried fruit resembling the trail mix.

---

### Candidate D — Chex Mix Traditional

| Field | Package Label | CSV | Match? |
|---|---|---|---|
| Product name | Chex Mix Traditional (Original Recipe) | Chex Mix Traditional | ✅ |
| Package size | **Printed weight unreadable** (front text is cropped/folded in the photo) | **8.75 oz** | ⚠️ **Cannot confirm** |
| Price | — | $4.99 | — |
| Servings/container | About 39 | — | — |
| Serving size | ½ cup (29 g) | — | — |
| Availability | — | In stock | — |

The printed net weight on the front of the bag is unreadable due to cropping. The nutrition panel lists "About 39" servings of 29 g each, which implies approximately 1,131 g (~39.9 oz) — inconsistent with the CSV's listed 8.75 oz. Because the printed weight cannot be read and the serving count appears inconsistent with the CSV size, the package-size match **cannot be confirmed**.

**Ingredients:** Whole wheat, degermed yellow corn meal, enriched flour (wheat flour, niacin, iron, thiamin mononitrate, riboflavin, folic acid), vegetable oil, sugar. Contains 2% or less of: salt, rye flour, maltodextrin, yeast, spices, color added, dextrose, distilled monoglycerides, baking soda, trisodium phosphate, yellow corn flour, calcium carbonate, autolyzed yeast, dried onion, dried garlic, hydrolyzed soy protein, barley malt syrup, citric acid, disodium inosinate, disodium guanylate, natural flavor. Freshness preserved by BHT.

**Allergen — Contains:** **Wheat** and soy ingredients.
**Allergen — Voluntary advisory:** None visible.

**Gluten-free on label?** **No.** Contains wheat, rye flour, and barley malt syrup — three gluten-grain sources.

**Certifications visible:** None.

**Cost per serving:** $4.99 ÷ 39 = **$0.13/serving** — **marked uncertain** because the serving count of 39 appears inconsistent with the CSV's 8.75 oz package size, and the printed net weight is unreadable.

**Trail-mix similarity:** Low — savory seasoned snack mix, no resemblance to the trail mix.

---

## 4. Summary Comparison

| Criterion | A: SkinnyPop | B: KIND Minis | C: Nature Valley | D: Chex Mix |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Price | $4.69 | $8.49 | $5.99 | $4.99 |
| Servings/container | 1 (uncertain) | 10 | 24 | ~39 (uncertain) |
| Cost/serving | $4.69 (uncertain) | $0.85 | $0.25 | $0.13 (uncertain) |
| Declares wheat? | No | No | No | **Yes** ⛔ |
| Gluten-free on label? | Uncertain | **Yes** ✅ | No | No |
| Package size matches CSV? | **No** ❌ | Yes ✅ | Yes ✅ | **Cannot confirm** ⚠️ |
| In stock? | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| Trail-mix similarity | Low | **Highest** | Low | Low |

---

## 5. Elimination & Selection

### Hard disqualifications

| Rule | Eliminated |
|---|---|
| Declares wheat | **D (Chex Mix)** — "Contains wheat and soy" |
| Package size mismatch or unconfirmed | **A (SkinnyPop)** — 1 oz photo vs 4.4 oz CSV; **D (Chex Mix)** — printed weight unreadable, serving count inconsistent with CSV |

### Remaining candidates: B (KIND) and C (Nature Valley)

| Check | B + C |
|---|---|
| Total cost | $8.49 + $5.99 = **$14.48** ≤ $18 ✅ |
| Wheat declared? | Neither declares wheat ✅ |
| ≥ 1 clearly says gluten-free? | B says "GLUTEN FREE" ✅ |
| Package sizes match CSV? | Both match ✅ |
| Combined servings | 10 + 24 = **34** |

### Per-person serving check (6 people)

| Requirement | B (KIND) | C (Nature Valley) | Combined |
|---|---|---|---|
| Servings available | 10 | 24 | 34 |
| Per person | 1.67 | 4.00 | 5.67 |
| ≥ 1 serving each? | ✅ | ✅ | — |
| ≥ 3 servings combined per person? | — | — | ✅ (5.67) |

All serving requirements satisfied for 6 people.

### Selection rationale

B + C is the **only qualifying combination** — the only pair that satisfies all hard rules simultaneously (in stock, ≤ $18, no wheat declared, matching package sizes, at least one gluten-free labeled).

### Relationship to Michelle's original snack plan

Michelle's trail-mix plan (Whole Foods, Feb 2026) was for a Bear Mountain hike, not an F1 watch party. The reference photo shows Power Up Mega Omega trail mix — a nut/seed/dried-fruit mix. Of the four candidates, **KIND Minis (B) is the most similar** thanks to its almond-and-peanut base with chocolate, echoing the nut-forward character of the trail mix. Nature Valley (C) provides the volume and value to ensure every guest has plenty to snack on during the race.

### Note on gluten safety for Candidate C

Nature Valley does **not** carry a gluten-free label. Its oats are not certified gluten-free. The selection rule required only that wheat not be *declared* and that *at least one* product clearly say gluten-free; C meets the "no wheat declared" rule but is **not** verified gluten-free. For any guest with celiac disease or strict gluten sensitivity, this should be communicated.

---

## 6. Relationship Between Occasions

| Occasion | Event | Relevant Finding |
|---|---|---|
| F1 Watch Parties | Bahrain (Mar 1) & Miami (May 3) | 6 attendees including Michelle; no dietary restrictions recorded |
| Bear Mountain Hike | Feb 15 | Trail mix from Whole Foods; separate outdoor activity |
| Tom's Birthday Dinner | Apr 18, The River Café | 1 gluten-free guest among dietary notes; separate private dining event |

The gluten-free requirement originates from the birthday dinner, not the F1 watch parties. No dietary restrictions were mentioned in any F1 watch-party communication. The trail-mix idea originated from a hiking trip, not F1 planning.

Everything else — the three occasions, 6-person attendance, Whole Foods, $0.85/$0.25 cost per serving, serving-per-person calculations, and $14.48 total — is unchanged.
